items.title: Drizzle on top: a new high-end dog food brand is coming for the 1%
items.summary: Golden Child is launching with two "five-star" products: a fresh frozen meal system and, more intriguingly, a "drizzle." It has also raised $37 million in funding.

items.title: India’s Snabbit closes $56M round as investor interest in on-demand home services heats up
items.summary: Snabbit now processes over 40,000 daily jobs and has cut costs sharply as it expands across cities and services.

items.title: Letterboxd, the social platform for film buffs, reportedly looking for new owner
items.summary: Potential buyers of Letterboxd include Versant, the parent company of CNBC and MS NOW, and Hollywood media company The Ankler, according to Semafor.

items.title: Consumers lost $2.1B to social media scams in 2025, FTC reports
items.summary: The agency reports that losses from social media scams have increased eightfold and that social media scams resulted in higher losses than any other method scammers used to contact consumers.

items.title: OpenAI ends Microsoft legal peril over its $50B Amazon deal
items.summary: OpenAI has won major concessions from its largest shareholder, Microsoft, that will allow it to sell products on AWS, while Microsoft gets more cash in a revenue-share agreement.

items.title: DeepMind’s David Silver just raised $1.1B to build an AI that learns without human data
items.summary: Ineffable Intelligence, a British AI lab founded a mere few months ago by former DeepMind researcher David Silver, has raised $1.1 billion in funding at a valuation of $5.1 billion.

items.title: Hacker who allegedly carried out cyberattacks for China is extradited to US
items.summary: Xu Zewei is accused of participating in a Chinese government hacking group that broke into thousands of U.S. organizations and stole COVID-19-related research.

items.title: Investors back Skye’s AI home screen app for iPhone ahead of launch
items.summary: Skye's new AI app attracted investors before it even launched — a sign of interest in a more AI-aware iPhone.

items.title: Data center demand drives 66% surge in natural gas power plant costs
items.summary: Natural gas power plant costs have nearly doubled in two years and take 23% longer to build as data center electricity demand skyrockets.

items.title: What’s behind Europe’s efforts to ditch US software in favor of sovereign tech
items.summary: Governments across Europe are looking to rely less on American tech providers.

items.title: China blocks Meta’s $2B Manus deal after months-long probe
items.summary: China has ordered Meta to unwind its multibillion-dollar Manus acquisition, dealing a potential setback to Zuckerberg’s push into AI agents.

items.title: OpenAI could be making a phone with AI agents replacing apps
items.summary: There have been plenty of rumors about OpenAI's hardware plans, which involve launching a pair of earbuds. A new note from industry analyst Ming-Chi Kuo suggests that the AI company might be working on a phone in collaboration with MediaTek, Qualcomm, and Luxshare.

items.title: Critical infrastructure giant Itron says it was hacked
items.summary: The American technology giant provides water and energy monitoring and utility meters to hundreds of millions of homes and businesses.

items.title: Spotify’s next frontier: Fitness content
items.summary: Spotify is adding fitness as its next major category, launching workout videos, playlists, and Peloton classes inside the app for free and Premium users.

items.title: Meta inks deal for solar power at night, beamed from space
items.summary: Overview Energy's first contract with Meta is a small step toward a future of space-based solar power.

items.title: Truecaller faces mounting pressures as its growth matures
items.summary: As growth slows, Truecaller is leaning on subscriptions, business services, and new features to sustain momentum beyond India.

items.title: The Stanford freshmen who want to rule the world … will probably read this book and try even harder
items.summary: Can a book like this actually change anything? Or does the spotlight, as it always seems to, send more students racing to the place?

items.title: Amazon’s new podcast strategy: Monetize everything
items.summary: Amazon's podcasting business seems to have transformed over the past six months.

items.title: What Tim Cook built
items.summary: On the latest episode of Equity, we discuss how Apple has changed since Cook became CEO in 2011, and what challenges incoming CEO John Ternus will be facing.

items.title: TechCrunch Mobility: Elon’s admission
items.summary: Welcome back to TechCrunch Mobility — your central hub for news and insights on the future of transportation.
